Scenario.title: Indian Critical Infrastructure Intrusion via Phishing and Network Compromise
Scenario.overview: Communications Intermediate A state-sponsored group targeting communications infrastructure exploited phishing attacks and software vulnerabilities to establish persistent access, escalate privileges, and disrupt operations using tactics aligned with RedEcho-style campaigns.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-23222 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested vulnerability in email client handling
stage.description: Attackers sent spear-phished emails with malicious .docm attachments containing macros to employees. When opened, the macros executed PowerShell commands to download a backdoor, exploiting poor employee training and basic security measures.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-41710 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested exploit for Microsoft Office macro bypass
stage.description: The malicious macro exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Office to execute arbitrary code, deploying a lightweight backdoor (e.g., Empire) onto the victim's endpoint to establish initial foothold.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A hidden scheduled task was created in Windows Task Scheduler to re-establish persistence across system reboots, ensuring continued access to the network despite temporary disruptions.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers leveraged default credentials in network devices (routers/switches) to escalate privileges, exploiting unpatched configurations to gain administrative control over critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: Payloads were encoded using base64 obfuscation and embedded in legitimate HTTP traffic to evade detection by basic antivirus solutions deployed in small-to-medium enterprises.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical routing tables were altered to redirect traffic, and server data was corrupted to mimic RedEcho tactics, disrupting communication services and causing operational downtime.

Scenario.summary: This intermediate-complexity attack began with a phishing campaign targeting communications sector employees, escalated through remote service exploitation, and culminated in data manipulation to disrupt infrastructure operations.
